% Helper function for 0-1 BFS implementation
% It returns the minimum number of heavy edges needed to reach target, or infinity if unreachable.
% N: number of nodes
% Adj: adjacency list structure
% source, target: start and end nodes
% k: max allowed heavy edges (used for early exit, but BFS finds minimum)
% T: current threshold
can_reach_min_heavy_edges(N, Adj, source, target, T) ->
    % D[i] stores the minimum heavy edge count to reach node i.
    % Initialize distances to infinity (represented by a large number or -1 if we handle reachability separately)
    % We use a map for distances keyed by node ID.
    D = maps:new(),
    % Deque for 0-1 BFS: [0-cost neighbors] ++ [1-cost neighbors]
    Deque = [source],
    maps:put(source, 0, D),

    dijkstra_01_bfs(Deque, D, target, T, N, Adj).

dijkstra_01_bfs([], D, target, _, _, _) ->
    % Queue empty, target unreachable or minimum cost already found.
    case maps:lookup(target, D) of
        {ok, cost} -> cost;
        error -> -1 % Represents infinity/unreachable
    end;

dijkstra_01_bfs([U|Rest], D, target, T, N, Adj) ->
    U_cost = maps:get(U, D),
    
    case Adj ! U of
        [] -> 
            % No neighbors, continue with rest of queue
            dijkstra_01_bfs(Rest, D, target, T, N, Adj);
        Neighbors ->
            % Process neighbors
            {NewDeque, NewD} = process_neighbors(U, U_cost, Neighbors, D, T, N, Adj, Rest),
            dijkstra_01_bfs(NewDeque, NewD, target, T, N, Adj).

% Helper to process all neighbors of U
process_neighbors(U, U_cost, Neighbors, D, T, N, Adj, Rest) ->
    foldl(process_edge, {Rest, D}, Neighbors).

% Helper to process a single edge (U, V) with weight W
process_edge({CurrentDeque, CurrentD}, [V, W], {NewDeque, NewD}) ->
    % Determine cost
    IsHeavy = (W > T),
    Cost = if IsHeavy -> 1; true -> 0 end,
    NewCost = U_cost + Cost,
    
    % Check if this path is better
    case maps:lookup(V, CurrentD) of
        {ok, OldCost} when NewCost < OldCost ->
            % Update distance and enqueue
            UpdatedD = maps:put(V, NewCost, CurrentD),
            NewDeque = if Cost == 0 -> [V|CurrentDeque]; true -> CurrentDeque ++ [V] end,
            {NewDeque, UpdatedD};
        _ -> 
            % No improvement, keep state
            {CurrentDeque, CurrentD}
    end;

% --- Main Solve Function ---

solve(N, Edges, source, target, K) ->
    % Edge case: source == target
    if source == target -> 0;
    true ->
        % 1. Build Adjacency List
        Adj = build_adj_list(N, Edges),
        
        % 2. Binary Search setup
        % Range of weights: [1, 10^9]. Threshold T can range from 0 up to 10^9.
        Low = 0,
        High = 1000000000, % 10^9
        
        % Initialize answer to -1 (unreachable)
        Answer = -1,

        % Binary Search loop
        binary_search(Low, High, Answer, N, Adj, source, target, K)
    end.

% Builds the undirected adjacency list
build_adj_list(N, Edges) ->
    % Initialize N empty lists (or map)
    InitialAdj = lists:duplicate(N, []),
    
    % Populate the adjacency list
    lists:foldl(add_edge, InitialAdj, Edges).

add_edge(Adj, [U, V, W]) ->
    % Add V to U's list and U to V's list
    NewAdj = lists:update_at(U, [[V, W] | lists:get_at(U, Adj)], Adj),
    lists:update_at(V, [[U, W] | lists:get_at(V, NewAdj)], NewAdj).

% Helper to update an element in a list (simulating array update)
lists:update_at(Index, NewValue, List) ->
    lists:replace_at(Index, NewValue, List).

% Helper to get an element from a list
lists:get_at(Index, List) ->
    case lists:replace_at(Index, undefined, List) of
        {_, Value} -> Value;
        error -> undefined
    end.

% Helper to replace an element in a list (used above)
lists:replace_at(Index, NewValue, List) ->
    case lists:replace_at(Index, undefined, List) of
        {_, OldValue} -> 
            lists:replace_at(Index, NewValue, List);
        error -> 
            {error}
    end.


% Binary Search implementation
binary_search(L, R, CurrentAnswer, N, Adj, source, target, K) ->
    if L > R ->
        CurrentAnswer;
    true ->
        M = L + (R - L) div 2,

        % Check if path exists for threshold M
        MinHeavyEdges = can_reach_min_heavy_edges(N, Adj, source, target, M),
        
        if MinHeavyEdges /= -1 and MinHeavyEdges <= K ->
            % Success: M is a possible threshold. Try smaller ones.
            NewAnswer = M,
            binary_search(L, M - 1, NewAnswer, N, Adj, source, target, K);
        else
            % Failure: M is too low. Need higher threshold.
            binary_search(M + 1, R, CurrentAnswer, N, Adj, source, target, K)
        end.
```

    maps:put(source, 0, D),

%%% Helper function to check if a path exists from source to target using at most K heavy edges, given a threshold T.
%%% Graph is expected to be an adjacency list: Adj[u] = list of {v, w}.
%%% State space: (node, heavy_count).
check_path(Adj, N, source, target, k, T) ->
    (source == target -> true;
     bfs_check(Adj, N, source, target, k, T)
    ).

%% Recursive BFS implementation
bfs_check(Adj, N, start_node, end_node, K, T) ->
    % Queue stores {node, heavy_count}
    % Visited map/array tracks {node, heavy_count}
    % Initialize: Q = [{start_node, 0}], Visited = {start_node, 0}
    bfs_loop([{start_node, 0}], [{start_node, 0}], K, T, Adj, end_node).

bfs_loop([], _, _, _, _, _) ->
    % Queue is empty, target not reached
    false.
bfs_loop([{U, J} | Queue], Visited, K, T, Adj, end_node) ->
    (U == end_node -> true;
     (
        % Process neighbors of U
        case lookup(U, Adj) of
            {Neighbors} ->
                % Generate next states
                NewStates = [
                    {V, J + (if W > T then 1 else 0)}
                    || {V, W} <- Neighbors
                    , J + (if W > T then 1 else 0) <= K
                ],
                % Filter out already visited states and update queue/visited list
                ValidNewStates = [S || S <- NewStates, not member(S, Visited)],
                
                % Update state lists
                NewQueue = Queue ++ ValidNewStates,
                NewVisited = Visited ++ ValidNewStates,
                
                % Continue BFS
                bfs_loop(NewQueue, NewVisited, K, T, Adj, end_node)
        ;
        % Should not happen if graph is built correctly
        bfs_loop(Queue, Visited, K, T, Adj, end_node)
     )
    ).


%% Main solver function
solve(N, Edges, source, target, K) ->
    % 1. Build Adjacency List (Graph)
    % Adj[u] = list of {v, w}
    Adj = build_graph(N, Edges),

    % 2. Handle source == target case
    (source == target -> 0;
     
     % 3. Collect all unique weights + 0 to define search space boundary
     AllWeights = [W || [_U, _V, W] <- Edges],
     UniqueWeights = lists:sort(lists:unique(AllWeights)),
     
     % Candidate thresholds: 0, and every weight w_i.
     % Binary search range: [L, R]. 
     % L=0 always possible (or maybe not, but we start here).
     % R = max weight + 1 (guarantees all edges are light).
     
     MaxW = if lists:empty(UniqueWeights) -> 0; lists:last(UniqueWeights) end,
     L = 0,
     R = MaxW + 1,
     
     % 4. Binary Search
     binary_search(L, R, -1, Adj, N, source, target, K)
    )

%% Helper to build adjacency list
build_graph(N, Edges) ->
    % Use an association list (key=node, value=list of {neighbor, weight})
    % Initialize empty list for all N nodes
    InitialAdj = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, U) -> [U-[] | Acc] end, [], 0..N-1),
    
    % Populate the list
    Adj = lists:foldl(fun(CurrentAdj, [U, V, W]) ->
        % Add edge (U, V, W) and (V, U, W) since the graph is undirected
        UpdateAdj(CurrentAdj, U, {V, W}),
        UpdateAdj(UpdateAdj(CurrentAdj, V, {U, W})),
        CurrentAdj
    end, InitialAdj, Edges).

%% Helper to update adjacency list
UpdateAdj(Adj, Node, Edge) ->
    case lookup(Node, Adj) of
        {Neighbors} -> 
            % Replace entry for Node with updated neighbors
            lists:replace(Adj, Node, [{Neighbors, Edge} | Neighbors]);
        _ -> 
            % Should not happen if initialization was correct
            Adj
    end.

%% Helper for finding unique elements while preserving order (used for simplicity, although sort/unique is fine)
lists:unique(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> 
        if not member(X, Acc) -> [X | Acc] ; Acc
    end, [], List).

%% Binary Search implementation
binary_search(L, R, BestResult, Adj, N, S, T_arg, K) ->
    (L > R -> BestResult;
     M = L + (R - L) div 2,
     
     % Check feasibility for threshold M
     IsFeasible = check_path(Adj, N, S, T_arg, K, M),
     
     (IsFeasible ->
        % M works, store it and try smaller thresholds
        binary_search(L, M - 1, M, Adj, N, S, T_arg, K)
     ;
        % M does not work, need larger thresholds
        binary_search(M + 1, R, BestResult, Adj, N, S, T_arg, K)
     )
    ).

%%% Graph is expected to be an adjacency list: Adj[u] = list of {v, w}.
%%% State space: (node, heavy_count).
check_path(Adj, N, source, target, k, T) ->
    (source == target -> true;
     bfs_check(Adj, N, source, target, k, T)
    ).

%% Recursive BFS implementation
bfs_check(Adj, N, start_node, end_node, K, T) ->
    % Queue stores {node, heavy_count}
    % Visited map/array tracks {node, heavy_count}
    % Initialize: Q = [{start_node, 0}], Visited = {start_node, 0}
    bfs_loop([{start_node, 0}], [{start_node, 0}], K, T, Adj, end_node).

bfs_loop([], _, _, _, _, _) ->
    % Queue is empty, target not reached
    false.
bfs_loop([{U, J} | Queue], Visited, K, T, Adj, end_node) ->
    (U == end_node -> true;
     (
        % Process neighbors of U
        case lookup(U, Adj) of
            {Neighbors} ->
                % Generate next states
                NewStates = [
                    {V, J + (if W > T then 1 else 0)}
                    || {V, W} <- Neighbors
                    , J + (if W > T then 1 else 0) <= K
                ],
                % Filter out already visited states and update queue/visited list
                ValidNewStates = [S || S <- NewStates, not member(S, Visited)],
                
                % Update state lists
                NewQueue = Queue ++ ValidNewStates,
                NewVisited = Visited ++ ValidNewStates,
                
                % Continue BFS
                bfs_loop(NewQueue, NewVisited, K, T, Adj, end_node)
        ;
        % Should not happen if graph is built correctly
        bfs_loop(Queue, Visited, K, T, Adj, end_node)
     )
    ).


%% Main solver function
solve(N, Edges, source, target, K) ->
    % 1. Build Adjacency List (Graph)
    % Adj[u] = list of {v, w}
    Adj = build_graph(N, Edges),

    % 2. Handle source == target case
    (source == target -> 0;
     
     % 3. Collect all unique weights + 0 to define search space boundary
     AllWeights = [W || [_U, _V, W] <- Edges],
     UniqueWeights = lists:sort(lists:unique(AllWeights)),
     
     % Candidate thresholds: 0, and every weight w_i.
     % Binary search range: [L, R]. 
     % L=0 always possible (or maybe not, but we start here).
     % R = max weight + 1 (guarantees all edges are light).
     
     MaxW = if lists:empty(UniqueWeights) -> 0; lists:last(UniqueWeights) end,
     L = 0,
     R = MaxW + 1,
     
     % 4. Binary Search
     binary_search(L, R, -1, Adj, N, source, target, K)
    )

%% Helper to build adjacency list
build_graph(N, Edges) ->
    % Use an association list (key=node, value=list of {neighbor, weight})
    % Initialize empty list for all N nodes
    InitialAdj = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, U) -> [U-[] | Acc] end, [], 0..N-1),

    Adj = lists:foldl(fun(CurrentAdj, [U, V, W]) ->
        % Add edge (U, V, W) and (V, U, W) since the graph is undirected
        UpdateAdj(CurrentAdj, U, {V, W}),
        UpdateAdj(UpdateAdj(CurrentAdj, V, {U, W})),
        CurrentAdj
    end, InitialAdj, Edges).

%% Helper to update adjacency list
UpdateAdj(Adj, Node, Edge) ->
    case lookup(Node, Adj) of
        {Neighbors} -> 
            % Replace entry for Node with updated neighbors
            lists:replace(Adj, Node, [{Neighbors, Edge} | Neighbors]);
        _ -> 
            % Should not happen if initialization was correct
            Adj
    end.

%% Helper for finding unique elements while preserving order (used for simplicity, although sort/unique is fine)
lists:unique(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> 
        if not member(X, Acc) -> [X | Acc] ; Acc
    end, [], List).

%% Binary Search implementation
binary_search(L, R, BestResult, Adj, N, S, T_arg, K) ->
    (L > R -> BestResult;
     M = L + (R - L) div 2,
     
     % Check feasibility for threshold M

    if Source == Target -> 0;
    true ->
        % 1. Preprocess graph into adjacency list: Adj[u] = list of {v, w}
        Adj = lists:nthtail(0, lists:foldl(fun(Acc, [u, v, w]) ->
            lists:insert(u, {v, w}, Acc);
            lists:insert(v, {u, w}, Acc)
        end, [[0, []] | lists:duplicate(N, [])], Edges)),

        % Helper function to check feasibility for a given threshold T
        check(T) ->
            % R[i][j] is true if node i is reachable using exactly j heavy edges.
            % Dimensions: N x (K+1)
            R = lists:duplicate(N, lists:duplicate(K + 1, false)),
            
            % Initialize BFS queue and reachability
            Queue = [{Source, 0}],
            R_init = lists:update(R, Source, lists:update(R !! Source, 0, true)),
            
            % BFS implementation
            bfs(Queue, R_init, Adj, K, Target);

        % BFS State: Queue (list of {node, heavy_count}), Reachability Matrix R
        bfs(Queue, R_matrix, Adj, K, Target) when is_list(Queue) ->
            case Queue of
                [] -> 
                    % Queue empty, check if target is reachable in any valid state
                    lists:any(fun(R_row) -> lists:any(fun(is_true) -> is_true end, R_row) end, lists:nthtail(Target, R_matrix));
                
                [{U, J} | Tail] ->
                    % Dequeue {U, J}
                    
                    % Find neighbors of U
                    Neighbors = lists:nth(U, Adj),
                    
                    NewQueue = Tail,
                    NewR_matrix = R_matrix,
                    
                    % Process neighbors
                    {NewQueue, NewR_matrix} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, {V, W}) ->
                        {CurrentQueue, CurrentR} = Acc;
                        
                        % Determine if edge (U, V) is heavy
                        Is_Heavy = W > T,
                        C_e = if Is_Heavy -> 1; true -> 0 end,
                        
                        J_prime = J + C_e,
                        
                        if J_prime <= K ->
                            % Check if R[V][J'] is false
                            is_reachable = lists:nth(J_prime, lists:nth(V, CurrentR)),
                            if not is_reachable ->
                                % Mark R[V][J'] = true
                                NewR_V = lists:update(CurrentR !! V, J_prime, true),
                                NextR = lists:update(CurrentR, V, NewR_V),

                                NextQueue = CurrentQueue ++ [{V, J_prime}],

                    bfs(NewQueue, NewR_matrix, Adj, K, Target)
